reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Implementation uses synchronous pruning optimization instead of explicit ancestry check. UpdateFinalizedCheckpoint (forkchoice.go:145-153) calls prune(ctx) synchronously under lock, removing all non-finalized-descending blocks from nodeByRoot map before returning. HasNode (line 158) then only returns true for blocks that survived pruning, which by construction descend from finalized checkpoint. While the spec suggests get_checkpoint_block ancestry walk, Prysm's approach achieves the same security guarantee via structural invariant (post-pruning, treeRootNode = finalizedNode). This is a valid implementation choice, not a vulnerability. Spec reference confirms ancestry requirement (SG-006 q_check_finalized_ancestor) but implementation satisfies it via pruning.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: SG-006 (fulu-p2p-interface) requires finalized checkpoint ancestry check via get_checkpoint_block; implementation achieves same via synchronous pruning
